articles.title: Macron calls for vigilance as western Europe faces second heatwave of year
articles.description: More than half of France’s population under severe weather warning with temperatures expected to exceed 40C

articles.title: PICS: Malawians arrive on home soil after Sherwood crisis
articles.description: Hundreds of Malawians displaced by alleged anti-migrant violence in South Africa have safely returned home under a voluntary repatriation programme

articles.title: Riley Norton and Vincent Tshituka feature in Bok leadership race
articles.description: Strong depth exists in the Springbok set-up, but the hunt for future skippers continues.

more.title: SAHRC Takes Ngizwe Mchunu to Johannesburg High Court Over LGBTQ+ Hate Speech
more.description: JOHANNESBURG, GAUTENG — The South African Human Rights Commission (SAHRC) is taking media personality Ngizwe Mchunu to the Johannesburg High Court over allegations of LGBTQ+ hate speech and incitement. The legal action stems from a series of controversial remarks made by Mchunu regarding same-sex couples, sparking a national debate on the balance between freedom of [...]

more.title: Hugo Broos ties Shakes Mashaba as ‘most-capped’ Bafana Bafana coach
more.description: Bafana Bafana coach Hugo Broos has tied Shakes Mashaba as the coach to have been in charge of the most matches of the South African national team.

more.title: R100k fine for employing illegal immigrants: What the law actually says
more.description: The R100 000 figure making headlines is not yet law. Here is how to tell the two apart. Two laws, one conversation If you have seen the R100 000 fine warning circulating online, you are not alone. The figure has been widely reported in recent weeks and while it is rooted in a real legislative development, there is an important... Read more → The post R100k fine for employing illegal immigrants: What the law actually says appeared first on Cape Town ETC .

more.title: The role of edge infrastructure in South Africa’s AI leap
more.description: The future of AI in South Africa is in local, distributed infrastructure closer to where data is created and used.
